topic.what: Randomised controlled trial evaluating Qirui Weishu capsule — a proprietary TCM compound preparation — for chronic atrophic gastritis (CAG), with pre-specified histological and symptomatic endpoints.
topic.how: Parallel-group RCT; patients with endoscopically and histologically confirmed CAG randomised to Qirui Weishu capsule vs. control (standard Western medicine or placebo); primary outcomes: Sydney System histological scores for atrophy, intestinal metaplasia, and inflammation at 3–6 months; secondary outcomes: TCM symptom scores, endoscopic appearance, and biomarker changes.
topic.why_matters: CAG carries pre-neoplastic risk and lacks approved disease-modifying treatments in Western medicine; demonstrating histological reversal in a well-controlled RCT would constitute high-quality evidence for TCM inclusion in CAG management guidelines.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Change in Sydney System gastric atrophy grade at follow-up endoscopy
characteristics.value: Statistically significant reduction in atrophy score in treatment arm vs. control
characteristics.evidence: Blinded histopathological scoring per Sydney System on biopsy specimens
characteristics.context: RCT design provides stronger causal inference than observational or animal data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Composite score of gastric pain, bloating, belching, and fatigue
characteristics.value: Significant superiority of Qirui Weishu over control on composite TCM symptom score
characteristics.evidence: Validated TCM symptom questionnaire administered at baseline and endpoint
characteristics.context: Symptom scores are patient-reported; blinding quality critical for interpreting subjective outcomes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Change in intestinal metaplasia grade on histology
characteristics.value: Modest but statistically significant improvement in metaplasia grade in treated group
characteristics.evidence: Blinded histopathology on repeat endoscopic biopsy
characteristics.context: Metaplasia reversal is harder to achieve than atrophy reversal; modest effect plausible for 3–6 month trial

good_for: Evidence-based TCM practitioners seeking RCT data for CAG management
Researchers designing future multi-centre trials for TCM CAG therapy
KB articles on highest-quality clinical evidence for TCM in gastric pre-neoplasia
DOI: 10.1016/j.jep.2022.115341 for full methods and data tables
not_for: H. pylori-negative CAG without stratified analysis
Advanced dysplasia or early gastric cancer
requires: Availability of Qirui Weishu capsule (proprietary preparation)
Endoscopic biopsy capability for histological outcome assessment

novelty: Provides rare RCT-level evidence for histological improvement in CAG with a TCM preparation, elevating TCM evidence from case series and small pilots
Uses internationally recognised Sydney System scoring, making findings interpretable outside TCM specialist community
open_problems: Composition and standardisation of Qirui Weishu capsule not publicly disclosed in full, limiting reproducibility
Long-term follow-up (>12 months) for gastric cancer incidence reduction not reported
Mechanistic basis for atrophy reversal not investigated in this trial
